ilFormulas.mitigationWhat: Armor / magic-resist damage mitigation. One generic curve for both physical armor and magic resist. Returns the damage-through multiplier applied to incoming damage.

mobDiff.what: The compiled function consuming MobDiffK/MobDiffPower. A mild, sub-linear, LEVEL-ONLY stat scaler applied to TValues flagged IsUseMobDiffK. NOT the boss difficulty band.

constants.source: Content/World/GameConstConfig.xml, read into GameConstsConfig at runtime

mobDiff.only_input: unit level (int TValue via ValueIntLevelName). There is NO difficulty-rank input in this path.

boss_band_verdict.reason: pow(x,0.1)=9.5 needs x~6.3e9, i.e. level~3e7 - impossible. MobDiffK cannot produce the band.
boss_band_verdict.true_origin_of_band: bespoke authored base stats in m_abs_* records (e.g. m_abs_woodraptor_young Hpb=28717.7); the band is a descriptive ratio (bespoke base / regular abstract-template curve), NOT a runtime multiplier.
boss_band_verdict.separate_boss_multiplier_search: negative - a full sweep of all 224100 methods and GameConstsConfig's 508 properties found NO BossMult/EliteMult/DifficultyMult/RankMult/GetDifficulty* anywhere; MobDiffK/MobDiffPower are the only mob-difficulty tunables in the binary.

evidence.pow_function: sub_1804F8340 (float->double log2/exp2 polynomial; confirmed two-arg power)

mobDiff.validation: Formula is the literal compiled arithmetic (direct read; constants known from GameConstConfig.xml; pow confirmed by disassembly). The task's specified anchor (x9-10 band) is deliberately NOT reproduced because MobDiffK is not that mechanism - disproof is evidenced, not force-fit.
mobDiff.action: monsters.json boss_difficulty_status 'unverified_lead' -> MobDiffK lead CLOSED (not the cause); boss band stays bespoke-base, no runtime multiplier.

itemQualityTierUpgrade.what: The three UniversalFormulas classes long suspected to hold 'the per-rarity/tier/upgrade multiplier tables'. They hold NO table. They are index accessors that surface the item's quality/tier/upgrade INDEX into the data-driven Formula engine.

returns.ParamItemQuality: item quality enum byte @[item+0x20] (Common/Uncommon/Rare/Epic = 0..3) as float

itemQualityTierUpgrade.arithmetic: each CalculateGen body's ONLY arithmetic is cvtdq2ps (int->float); zero mulss, zero divss, zero .rdata float-table lookup.
itemQualityTierUpgrade.implication: no compiled per-rarity/tier/upgrade multiplier table exists in these classes (or, per the mob_diff sweep, anywhere as a named constant table). Any magnitude scaling is authored data-driven in Entities.dat Formula trees / tear-tables, where tier is a first-class roll axis and rarity gates secondary-slot count.
itemQualityTierUpgrade.validation: Direct disassembly of all three bodies; confirms mechanism, not a number.

provenance.weightShare: Loot 'ws' (and the legacy 'ch' alias the current front still reads) is a within-table weight share — item weight ÷ table total weight, a RELATIVE likelihood. It is NOT a server-side true drop chance, which the client does not carry.
provenance.questFollowability: Quest-goal target followability tokens are neutral: join/prov 'inferred' means a name-fold (never a client GUID crosslink); state 'dynamic' = server-decided/dynamic spawn, state 'unknown' = no locatable client data.
provenance.campRoster: Camp roster.state 'server' = the exact per-camp roster is server-decided (no member list); 'candidates' = listed members are inferred family-token matches. Co-spawn families are 'probable', never asserted alongside the placed family.
provenance.referenceBinNames: specializations / professions / talents Name+Description are re-joined per locale from the localization index (EN is the pivot; a row with no localized text keeps the EN fallback). creeps display names still fold from the pivot locale (EnUs) pending a per-key Units.xml re-join -- 'nameFallback' flags those rows so a front badge can show it.

excludedPlacements: mo_ (AI pathing waypoints) and hs_ (arena event anchors) static placements are intentionally excluded from every map pin layer, as are quest_/mark_/qap_ engine anchors — they are not interactable content.
statTemplatesNote: per-level HP/stat extrapolation (`curve`) removed — it was computed from a misread field (HpL), ~640x too low on a boss; tier/level/stats kept as each template's own honest reference reading only, never a per-mob estimate. See data/SCHEMA.md 'Monster stats'.

bossDifficulty.note: live boss HP = bespoke base Hpb x server difficulty multiplier; the multiplier is NOT client-encoded (no HP formula consumes a difficulty/BDL/tier param) -- these are the observed magnitudes, at the boss's design encounter (level ~20).

client_difficulty_ranks.BDL: Boss Difficulty Level -- bare label, values 1..3 here, NO formula consumes it
client_difficulty_ranks.ladder_status: the full multiplier ladder is server-side, not a client table
